title: Project capstone: interactive game architecture
summary: Use the arcade-game chapters as a systems-design capstone for loops, state updates, events, classes, and progressive refactoring.

items.prompt: What does a game loop repeatedly do?

solution.summary: It processes input, updates the game state, and redraws the scene over and over until the program ends.

items.prompt: Why is it helpful to separate rendering code from state-update code in a game project?

solution.summary: Separating update and rendering responsibilities makes the code easier to debug, test, and extend.

items.prompt: If you were planning the arcade project from scratch, what subsystems would you define first?

solution.summary: Thinking in subsystems helps a toy project scale into a maintainable architecture.
checklist: World state: player, enemies, bullets, score.
Update rules for movement and collisions.
